concerns.text: Tasks T203, T204, T207 (Experimental Validation) and T205, T206 (Conformational Ensembles/Hydration) are placed in Phase 8 but depend on artifacts (experimental ground truth, multi-conformer data) that are explicitly declared 'out-of-scope' in spec.md Assumptions and FR-011. This creates a logical impossibility: the tasks require inputs that the project constitution and spec forbid generating or sourcing. A consumer task cannot precede a producer that is explicitly prohibited from existing.

Tasks T200, T201, T208 (Physics-Informed Loss/Geometric Constraints) and T202, T209 (Electronegativity Features) are ordered after the model training tasks (T026-T034) in Phase 4, yet they fundamentally alter the model architecture (FR-004) and feature set (FR-003) defined in Phase 2/3. Implementing these changes in Phase 8 implies re-running the entire training pipeline (T028, T029) with new architectures, but the task list does not include re-training tasks. This violates the 'Producer before Consumer' rule: the new model definitions (producers) are not established before the evaluation tasks (consumers) that rely on them.

Task T205 (Conformational Sampling) is listed in Phase 8, but it is a data-generation task that must precede T017 (3D Coordinate Extraction) and T018 (2D Descriptor Generation) in Phase 3. If T205 is executed, the existing data pipeline (T016-T020) becomes invalid as it assumes single-conformer input. The current ordering places the data producer (T205) after the data consumers (T017-T020), breaking the data-flow dependency.

Phase 8 is labeled 'Reviewer Revision - Physical Constraints & Validation' but contains tasks that contradict the spec's scope. While the ordering is the primary failure, the phase title suggests these are optional revisions, yet the tasks are marked as mandatory implementation steps without a clear 'if-then' dependency structure. This ambiguity makes the execution order undefined.
